description: Deck Builder UI Overhaul - Multi-screen deck management with visual card grid, persistent storage, and graphical magicka curve

userStories.title: Create DeckPersistence module for saving/loading decks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure data module to persist user decks to user://decks/ so that other modules can save and load decks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/deck/deck_persistence.gd with class_name DeckPersistence
Implement static save_deck(deck_name: String, definition: Dictionary) that writes JSON to user://decks/<sanitized_name>.json
Implement static load_deck(deck_name: String) -> Dictionary that reads and parses the JSON file
Implement static list_decks() -> Array[String] that returns all saved deck names
Implement static delete_deck(deck_name: String) -> bool that removes the file
Deck definition format includes name, attribute_ids array, and cards array with card_id/quantity entries
Creates user://decks/ directory if it does not exist
Loading a non-existent deck returns an empty dictionary
userStories.notes: Pure data module with no UI dependencies. Uses Godot FileAccess and DirAccess for user:// path.

userStories.title: Add DeckPersistence tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for DeckPersistence to verify save/load round-trips and edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/deck_persistence_runner.gd following existing test runner pattern
Test save then load round-trip returns identical deck definition
Test list_decks returns correct deck names after saving multiple decks
Test delete_deck removes the deck and it no longer appears in list_decks
Test loading a non-existent deck returns an empty dictionary
Test saving overwrites an existing deck with the same name
userStories.notes: Follow pattern in tests/deck_validation_runner.gd. Tests exercise file I/O against user:// storage.

userStories.title: Add deckbuilder flags to CardDisplayComponent
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CardDisplayComponent to support disabling hover effects, showing a quantity badge, and greying out at copy limit for deckbuilder use.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a set_interactive(enabled: bool) method that disables/enables hover effects and particle effects
Add a set_deck_quantity(current: int, max_copies: int) method that shows a quantity badge overlay (e.g. '2/3') on the card
When current >= max_copies, the card imagery is greyed out via modulate
When set_interactive(false), no hover visual changes occur on mouse enter/exit
Quantity badge is a small label positioned on the card (e.g. top-right corner)
Existing PRESENTATION_FULL mode rendering is unaffected when these features are not used
userStories.notes: Minimal additive changes to the existing 1036-line CardDisplayComponent.gd. Do not refactor existing code.

userStories.title: Create MagickaCurveChart widget
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a self-contained widget that renders a magicka cost distribution bar chart for a deck.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ui/components/magicka_curve_chart.gd with class_name MagickaCurveChart extending Control
Implement set_deck(definition: Dictionary, card_by_id: Dictionary) that computes cost bucket counts
Renders 8 vertical bars for cost buckets 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7+
Bar heights are proportional to card count in each bucket
Bars are blue accent colour (approximately Color(0.42, 0.62, 0.96))
Count labels displayed above or on each bar
Cost labels (0-6, 7+) displayed below each bar
Built programmatically with no .tscn file
userStories.notes: Pure widget with simple interface. Bucket calculation: cost 7+ grouped together. Uses _draw() or child Control nodes.

userStories.title: Add MagickaCurveChart bucket calculation tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the magicka curve bucket calculation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/magicka_curve_chart_runner.gd following existing test runner pattern
Test empty deck returns all zero buckets
Test single card at cost 3 returns count 1 in bucket 3 and 0 elsewhere
Test cards at cost 7, 8, 9, 10 all count in the 7+ bucket
Test multiple cards with quantities are counted correctly
Test a realistic deck with mixed costs produces expected distribution
userStories.notes: Test the data transformation, not the visual rendering. May need to expose bucket calculation as a static or public method.

userStories.title: Create DeckCreationModal for name and attribute selection
userStories.description: As a player, I want to create a new deck by entering a name and selecting 1-3 attributes in a modal dialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ui/deck_creation_modal.gd with class_name DeckCreationModal extending Control or Popup
Modal contains a LineEdit for deck name input
Modal contains 5 attribute toggle buttons (Strength, Intelligence, Willpower, Agility, Endurance)
Minimum 1 attribute must be selected, maximum 3
When 3 attributes are selected, remaining unselected buttons are disabled
Confirm button is disabled when name is empty or no attributes are selected
Emits a signal on confirm with deck name and selected attribute_ids
Emits a signal on cancel
Built programmatically with no .tscn file
userStories.notes: This modal is reused for editing deck name/attributes from the editor screen. Editing mode is handled in a later story.

userStories.title: Create DeckListScreen with deck management
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a list of my saved decks with options to create new, edit, or delete decks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ui/deck_list_screen.gd with class_name DeckListScreen extending Control
Displays a list of saved deck names loaded via DeckPersistence.list_decks()
Each deck entry is clickable and emits a signal to edit that deck
Each deck entry has a delete button
Delete button shows a confirmation dialog before deleting via DeckPersistence.delete_deck()
Create New Deck button opens the DeckCreationModal
On DeckCreationModal confirm, saves a new empty deck via DeckPersistence and emits edit signal
When no decks exist, only the Create New Deck button is shown
Built programmatically with no .tscn file
userStories.notes: This is the entry point screen. Navigation to/from the editor is handled by signal emission.

userStories.title: Build DeckEditorScreen left column - filter bar with toggle chips and dropdowns
userStories.description: As a player, I want to filter cards by attribute chips, cost chips, and class/type/keyword dropdowns.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ui/deck_editor_screen.gd with class_name DeckEditorScreen extending Control
Left column contains a filter bar at the top
Search bar (LineEdit) for name search at the top of the filter area
Attribute filter uses toggle chip buttons (Strength, Intelligence, Willpower, Agility, Endurance, Neutral) - multiple can be active
Cost filter uses toggle chip buttons (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7+) - multiple can be active
Class, Type, and Keyword filters use OptionButton dropdowns
Attribute chips and other filters are visually separated (e.g. by spacing or a separator)
Filters correctly narrow the card list using existing filter logic from DeckbuilderScreen
Built programmatically with no .tscn file
userStories.notes: Reuse filter logic from existing DeckbuilderScreen. Attribute and cost chips at top/bottom, dropdowns between.

userStories.title: Build DeckEditorScreen left column - card grid with CardDisplayComponent
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see filtered cards as a 3-column grid of full card displays that scale to fill space.
acceptanceCriteria: Below the filter bar, a ScrollContainer contains a GridContainer with 3 columns
Each cell contains a CardDisplayComponent in PRESENTATION_FULL mode
Cards scale to fill available column width while maintaining aspect ratio
CardDisplayComponent instances have hover effects disabled via set_interactive(false)
Quantity badges show current copies in deck (e.g. '1/3') on each card
Cards at copy limit are greyed out
Neutral cards always appear regardless of deck attribute selection
Single-clicking a card adds one copy to the deck (if not at limit)
Grid updates when filters change or cards are added/removed from deck
userStories.notes: The grid should reuse CardDisplayComponent. Cards need click handling via input_event or a clickable wrapper.

userStories.title: Build DeckEditorScreen right column - deck header and compact card list
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my deck name, attributes, and a compact sorted card list with remove buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Right column header shows deck name and attribute icons
Clicking the header opens DeckCreationModal in edit mode with current name and attributes pre-filled
When attributes are removed in edit mode and cards would become illegal, a confirmation dialog warns the user
On confirm, illegal cards are removed from the deck
Below the header, a scrollable list shows deck cards sorted by cost ascending
Each row contains: cost badge (small colored circle with cost number), card name, quantity indicator (e.g. 'x3'), minus button
Minus button removes one copy of that card from the deck
List updates when cards are added or removed
userStories.notes: Cost badge should use attribute tint colour or a neutral colour. Sorting by cost then alphabetically within same cost.

userStories.title: Build DeckEditorScreen right column - magicka curve and card count
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a magicka curve bar chart and colour-coded card count below my deck list.
acceptanceCriteria: MagickaCurveChart widget is placed below the deck card list
Chart updates whenever cards are added or removed
Card count label shows current count (e.g. '35 / 50-100')
Card count text is red when deck size is below minimum or above maximum
Card count text is white when deck size is within valid range
Validation uses DeckValidator with current deck definition and DeckRulesRegistry
userStories.notes: Reuse existing DeckValidator.validate_deck() for determining validity.

userStories.title: Add Done and Cancel buttons with save/discard behaviour
userStories.description: As a player, I want Done to save my deck and Cancel to discard changes, both returning to the deck list.
acceptanceCriteria: Done button at bottom-right of the deck panel saves the deck via DeckPersistence.save_deck()
Cancel button is visually distinct from Done (e.g. different colour, outline style)
Cancel button discards all changes made during the editing session
Both buttons emit a signal to navigate back to DeckListScreen
Done saves even if deck is invalid (user may want to come back and finish later)
The original deck state is preserved on entry so Cancel can restore it
userStories.notes: Store a snapshot of the deck definition on editor entry for cancel/discard.

userStories.title: Wire up screen navigation between DeckListScreen and DeckEditorScreen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to navigate between the deck list and deck editor seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: A parent controller or the screens themselves handle navigation via signals
Selecting a deck on DeckListScreen loads it and shows DeckEditorScreen
Creating a new deck on DeckListScreen saves it empty and shows DeckEditorScreen
Done or Cancel on DeckEditorScreen returns to DeckListScreen
DeckListScreen refreshes its deck list when returning from the editor
The existing deckbuilder_screen.gd entry point is replaced with the new flow
userStories.notes: Simplest approach: a parent node that swaps visibility of the two screens, or add_child/remove_child.
